params.name: response_type
params.description: Specifies the authorization flow. Use 'code' for Authorization Code Flow, 'id_token' for Implicit Flow.

params.name: client_id
params.description: The client identifier registered with the OpenID Provider

params.name: redirect_uri
params.description: URI to redirect to after authentication. Must match a pre-registered redirect URI for the client.

params.name: scope
params.description: Space-separated list of scopes. Must include 'openid'. Additional scopes: profile, email, address, phone, offline_access.

params.name: state
params.description: Opaque value used to maintain state between the request and callback. Used to prevent CSRF attacks.

params.name: nonce
params.description: String value used to associate a client session with an ID token and mitigate replay attacks.

params.name: code_challenge
params.description: PKCE code challenge derived from the code_verifier. Required for public clients using PKCE.

params.name: code_challenge_method
params.description: Method used to derive the code_challenge from code_verifier

params.name: response_mode
params.description: Mechanism used to return authorization response parameters

params.name: prompt
params.description: Prompts for user interaction during authentication

params.name: login_hint
params.description: Hint to the authorization server about the login identifier

items.docs: Initiates an OpenID Connect authorization flow. The client redirects the user to this endpoint with the required parameters. On successful authentication, the OpenID Provider redirects back to the client's redirect_uri with an authorization code (Authorization Code Flow) or tokens (Implicit Flow).

Exchanges an authorization code for an access token, ID token, and optionally a refresh token. Also used to refresh expired access tokens using a refresh token. Supports client_secret_basic and client_secret_post authentication methods.
Revokes an access token or refresh token. The token is immediately invalidated and can no longer be used for API access or token refresh.
Returns claims about the authenticated user. The access token is passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. The claims returned depend on the scopes granted during authorization.
Returns claims about the authenticated user via POST request. The access token is passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

Returns the OpenID Provider's configuration metadata as defined in RFC 8414 (OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server Metadata) and the OIDC Discovery specification. Clients use this endpoint to discover all required endpoints, supported features, and public keys.

Returns the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) containing the public keys used by the OpenID Provider to sign ID tokens and other JWTs. Clients use these keys to verify the signature of ID tokens received from the token endpoint.
